jobs.title: HR Business Partner
jobs.description:
HR Business Partner — MKS PAMP SA, Castel San Pietro (TI).

MISSION The mission of this role is to provide HR services in support of the company’s growth, performance, and international development. The position reports to the Head of Human Resources and acts as a regular business partner to the management while ensuring operational excellence, robust governance, and scalable people processes across the organization. MAIN ACTIVITIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES HR PARTNERSHIP Partner with the area management to support business growth objectives and build aligned people strategies. Advise managers on organizational design, succession and workforce planning, leadership development, engagement, communication, conflict management, team effectiveness, change management, etc. Provide data-driven insights to support decision-making and improve overall organizational performance. TALENT ACQUISITION AND DEVELOPMENT Actively drive end-to-end recruitment processes on the global ATS to ensure critical roles are filled quickly and with the right quality Monitor recruitment pipelines, bottlenecks, and time-to-fill metrics, and intervene when performance deviates from expectations Use data and dashboards to make delays visible and drive accountability Support the identification and development of talent and future leaders through structured programs, training, and mentoring. Lead locally annual Global talent management processes: performance reviews, goal setting, succession planning, and talent reviews. Provide support to local HR teams in their area. HR OPERATIONS & COMPLIANCE Ensure consistent application of global HR policies, processes, and compliance requirements. Partner with local HR teams to deliver seamless HR operations. Promote a positive, inclusive, and performance-driven culture across global teams. Contribute to compensation reviews and bonus allocation. COMPENSATION, BENEFITS AND MOBILITY Support compensation and bonus processes, including data consolidation, analysis, and communication. Support international assignments and global mobility processes Oversee payroll processes in partnership with the accounting department. TECHNOLOGY AND HR INFORMATION SYSTEMS Ensure effective use of HRIS, ATS, LMs platforms, people data, and HR technology as decision-support tools for leaders and managers Act as the regional business owner for HR data quality, usage, and interpretation, in close coordination with HR Operations and relevant COEs Ensure that people decisions (performance, talent, succession, workforce planning, compensation) are supported by reliable data and consistent reporting Ensure data quality, system usage, and provide reporting that supports governance and decision credibility Contribute to the rollout and adoption of global HRIS and HR technology initiatives at regional level, ensuring business relevance and leader adoption Use people analytics to identify risks, trends, and priorities, and translate insights into concrete leadership actions Promote a data-driven and automation-minded culture within HR teams and with managers Identify opportunities where technology can simplify, accelerate, standardize, or eliminate non–value-added HR activities HR PROJECTS AND PROGRAMMES As a member of the Group HR team, participate in or manage projects related to L&D, employee engagement, ESG and other initiatives Facilitate effective project management and execution in close partnership with local Human Resources teams. REQUIRED EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business, or related field; Master’s degree preferred. 6+ years HRBP experience in international environments. Strong knowledge of global HR practices and Swiss labor regulations Demonstrated expertise in talent management, workforce planning, and HR operations. QUALIFICATIONS AND SKILLS Excellent communication skills and high drive; Excellent interpersonal skills and customer orientation; Sense of responsibility and initiative; Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail; Ability to work autonomously; Demonstrated track record of continuous improvement and innovative thinking in approach and in practical effect; Resistance to stress and time-pressure; Agile and flexible mindset, reactive to requests; Ability to interact with colleagues of diverse cultures; respect for diversity; Full professional proficiency in Office 365 is expected. Full professional proficiency in spoken and written English and Italian is required; knowledge of French is an asset. Swiss national, EU national, or holder of a valid Swiss work permit.

jobs.title: Idraulico Manutentore Macchinari Industriali
jobs.description:
Idraulico Manutentore Macchinari Industriali — MKS PAMP SA, Castel San Pietro (TI).

MISSIONE La risorsa sarà inserita all’interno del reparto Maintenance di MKS PAMP. PRINCIPALI ATTIVITÀ E RESPONSABILITÀ Eseguire lavori manuali di installazione, montaggio e manutenzione su impianti/macchinari per la parte idraulica; Eseguire lavori su impianti aspirazione in materiale plastico ( PP, PPS etc ); Presa in carico degli ordini di lavorazione da piattaforma informatica (tablet o PC); Registrazione lavoro completato (su tablet o PC); Verifica lo stato degli impianti prima e dopo l’utilizzo, riportando eventuali anomalie al responsabile; Assistenza a ditte esterne ( elettriche , idrauliche e meccaniche); Collaborare con il dipartimento di manutenzione per pianificare e programmare le attività di manutenzione correttiva/straordinaria; Lettura e interpretazione schemi idraulici e pneumatici FORMAZIONE ED ESPERIENZA RICHIESTE Manutentore con formazione o esperienza idrauliche su macchinari industriali Completano il profilo eventuali diplomi in ambito idraulico Saldatura di plastica con polifusore.pistola ad aria calda Incollaggio tubazioni plastiche ( PVC, PVCC ecc ) Conoscenza e utilizzo di attrezzature da officina Conoscenza ed eventuale utilizzo macchinari da officina (trapano, tornio, fresa,piegatrice ecc.) Patente Svizzera uso carrelli elevatori / piattaforma mobili a braccio 3B QUALIFICHE E COMPETENZE Senso di responsabilità e iniziativa; Capacità di lavorare autonomamente; Resistenza allo stress, alla pressione del tempo e capacità di far fronte ai periodi di massima attività; Eccellenti capacità di problem solving e attenzione ai dettagli; Mentalità agile e flessibile, reattiva alle richieste; Capacità di interagire con colleghi di diverse culture; rispetto per la diversità; Competenze informatiche; ci si aspetta una buona conoscenza di Windows; Lavoro su tre turni ; Cittadino svizzero o dellUE o titolare di un permesso di lavoro svizzero valido

descriptionByLocale.de:
**Idrauliker für Industrieanlagen - MKS PAMP SA, Castel San Pietro (TI)**

**MISSION** Die Ressource wird innerhalb des Bereichs Maintenance von MKS PAMP eingebunden werden.

**HAUPTSÄCHLICHE TÄTIGKEITEN UND VERANTWORTUNGEN** Durchführen von handwerklichen Arbeiten zur Installation, Montage und Wartung von Anlagen/Maschinen im Bereich Hydraulik; Durchführen von Arbeiten an Anlagen für die Aspirationsmaterialien (PP, PPS usw.); Übernahme von Aufträgen zur Bearbeitung über die Plattform (Tablet oder PC); Registrieren des abgeschlossenen Arbeitsvorgangs (auf Tablet oder PC); Überprüfen des Zustand der Anlagen vor und nach dem Einsatz und melden etwaige Anomalien an den Verantwortlichen; Unterstützung externer Unternehmen (elektrische, hydraulische und mechanische); Zusammenarbeit mit dem Abteilung für Wartung, um die Aktivitäten der korrigierenden/außergewöhnlichen Wartung zu planen und zu programmieren; Lesen und Interpretieren von hydraulischen und pneumatischen Schemata

**BILDUNG UND ERFAHRUNG** Wartungstechniker mit hydraulischer Ausbildung oder Erfahrung auf Industrieanlagen; Ergänzt das Profil: Abschluss in hydraulischem Bereich; Schweißen von Kunststoff mit Polifusor und Pistole mit heißer Luft; Kleben von Kunststoffrohren (PVC, PVCC usw.); Kenntnisse und Verwendung von Werkzeugen aus der Werkstatt; Kenntnisse und mögliche Verwendung von Werkzeugen aus der Werkstatt (Bohrmaschine, Drehmaschine, Fräsmaschine, Falzmaschine usw.); Schweizer Führerschein für Fahrzeuge mit Aufzug oder Plattform mit 3B-Arme

**QUALIFIKATIONEN UND FÄHIGKEITEN** Verantwortungsbewusstsein und Initiative; Fähigkeit, autonom zu arbeiten; Widerstandsfähigkeit gegenüber Stress, Zeitdruck und Fähigkeit, sich auf Spitzenzeiten einzustellen; Exzellente Problemlösungsfähigkeit und Aufmerksamkeit auf Details; Flexibilität und Reaktionsfähigkeit auf Anforderungen; Fähigkeit, mit Kollegen aus verschiedenen Kulturen zu interagieren; Respekt für Vielfalt; IT-Kenntnisse; eine gute Kenntnis von Windows wird erwartet; Arbeit in drei Schichten; Schweizer Bürger oder EU-Bürger oder Inhaber eines gültigen Arbeitsvisums für die Schweiz

jobs.title: Metal & Inventory Controller
jobs.description:
Metal & Inventory Controller — MKS PAMP SA, Castel San Pietro (TI).

MISSION The Metal & Inventory Controller is responsible for ensuring robust control design and operating effectiveness across metal and material movements, inventory accuracy, and related processes within the factory environment. Acting as a second line of control, the role independently reviews, validates, and tests material flows and inventory transactions to ensure compliance with internal policies and procedures. The position contributes to continuous improvement of inventory and movement controls, supports bi-annual physical inventory counts, and provides actionable insights to strengthen operational integrity, transparency, and risk mitigation. MAIN ACTIVITIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES Control Design & Operating Effectiveness Support in design, document, audit and maintain controls related to metal/material movements and inventory tracking. Conduct regular reviews of control operating effectiveness, identifying gaps or weaknesses. Perform detailed tests of movements and adjustments to confirm adherence to documented procedures. Coordinate with production, operations and logistics to clarify movement discrepancies and enforce control standards. Inventory Monitoring & Reconciliation Monitor inbound/outbound metal movements and validate accuracy in inventory systems. Reconcile inventory records with physical counts and system transactions to identify variances. Perform cause analysis of discrepancies and coordinate corrective actions. Support cycle count programs and lead investigations of root causes in count differences. Physical Inventory Support Support to plan, execute and document physical inventory counts. Support to prepare inventory count procedures, control test criteria, and reports. Process Improvement & Governance Propose improvements to policies, procedures, and system controls to strengthen inventory accuracy and control design. Collaborate with Production, Logistics, and IT teams to streamline processes and reduce error rates. Review process flows, controls, and training material for physical movements of metals. Reporting & Stakeholder Engagement Prepare reports on inventory accuracy, control deviations, test results, and improvement initiatives. Act as key interface between quality assurance, operations, IT and factory leadership on metal movement / inventory and control issues. Support internal/external audits with evidence, reconciliations, and control design documentation. REQUIRED EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE 5–10 years of relevant experience in Industrial Engineering, Supply Chain Management, Quality Management, or Logistics. Proven experience in inventory control, material control, or a related function within a manufacturing or factory environment. Bachelor’s degree in Industrial Engineering, Supply Chain Management, Quality Management, or Logistics (preferred). Demonstrated experience in control design, inventory reconciliation, and process improvement. Hands-on experience with ERP and inventory management systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, or equivalent). QUALIFICATIONS, SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE Italian speaking is mandatory, conversational in English Strong analytical, investigative, and problem-solving capability. Excellent attention to detail and accuracy in reporting and data reviews. Ability to work independently, prioritize tasks, and communicate findings clearly across functions. Knowledge of quality control standards and best practices (e.g., ISO 9001 or similar frameworks) is a plus. Proactive mindset with a focus on continuous improvement and risk mitigation.

Process Improvement & Governance Propose improvements to policies, procedures, and system controls to strengthen inventory accuracy and control design. Collaborate with Production, Logistics, and IT teams to streamline processes and reduce error rates. Review process flows, controls, and training material for physical movements of metals. Reporting & Stakeholder Engagement Prepare reports on inventory accuracy, control deviations, test results, and improvement initiatives. Act as key interface between quality assurance, operations, IT and factory leadership on metal movement / inventory and control issues. Support internal/external audits with evidence, reconciliations, and control design documentation. REQUIRED EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE 5–10 years of relevant experience in Industrial Engineering, Supply Chain Management, Quality Management, or Logistics. Proven experience in inventory control, material control, or a related function within a manufacturing or factory environment. Bachelor’s degree in Industrial Engineering, Supply Chain Management, Quality Management, or Logistics (preferred). Demonstrated experience in control design, inventory reconciliation, and process improvement. Hands-on experience with ERP and inventory management systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, or equivalent). QUALIFICATIONS, SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE Italian speaking is mandatory, conversational in English Strong analytical, investigative, and problem-solving capability. Excellent attention to detail and accuracy in reporting and data reviews. Ability to work independently, prioritize tasks, and communicate findings clearly across functions. Knowledge of quality control standards and best practices (e.g., ISO 9001 or similar frameworks) is a plus. Proactive mindset with a focus on continuous improvement and risk mitigation.
descriptionByLocale.en:
Metal & Inventory Controller — MKS PAMP SA, Castel San Pietro (TI).

MISSION The Metal & Inventory Controller is responsible for ensuring robust control design and operating effectiveness across metal and material movements, inventory accuracy, and related processes within the factory environment. Acting as a second line of control, the role independently reviews, validates, and tests material flows and inventory transactions to ensure compliance with internal policies and procedures. The position contributes to continuous improvement of inventory and movement controls, supports bi-annual physical inventory counts, and provides actionable insights to strengthen operational integrity, transparency, and risk mitigation. MAIN ACTIVITIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES Control Design & Operating Effectiveness Support in design, document, audit and maintain controls related to metal/material movements and inventory tracking. Conduct regular reviews of control operating effectiveness, identifying gaps or weaknesses. Perform detailed tests of movements and adjustments to confirm adherence to documented procedures. Coordinate with production, operations and logistics to clarify movement discrepancies and enforce control standards. Inventory Monitoring & Reconciliation Monitor inbound/outbound metal movements and validate accuracy in inventory systems. Reconcile inventory records with physical counts and system transactions to identify variances. Perform cause analysis of discrepancies and coordinate corrective actions. Support cycle count programs and lead investigations of root causes in count differences. Physical Inventory Support Support to plan, execute and document physical inventory counts. Support to prepare inventory count procedures, control test criteria, and reports. Process Improvement & Governance Propose improvements to policies, procedures, and system controls to strengthen inventory accuracy and control design. Collaborate with Production, Logistics, and IT teams to streamline processes and reduce error rates. Review process flows, controls, and training material for physical movements of metals. Reporting & Stakeholder Engagement Prepare reports on inventory accuracy, control deviations, test results, and improvement initiatives. Act as key interface between quality assurance, operations, IT and factory leadership on metal movement / inventory and control issues. Support internal/external audits with evidence, reconciliations, and control design documentation. REQUIRED EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE 5–10 years of relevant experience in Industrial Engineering, Supply Chain Management, Quality Management, or Logistics. Proven experience in inventory control, material control, or a related function within a manufacturing or factory environment. Bachelor’s degree in Industrial Engineering, Supply Chain Management, Quality Management, or Logistics (preferred). Demonstrated experience in control design, inventory reconciliation, and process improvement. Hands-on experience with ERP and inventory management systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, or equivalent). QUALIFICATIONS, SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE Italian speaking is mandatory, conversational in English Strong analytical, investigative, and problem-solving capability. Excellent attention to detail and accuracy in reporting and data reviews. Ability to work independently, prioritize tasks, and communicate findings clearly across functions. Knowledge of quality control standards and best practices (e.g., ISO 9001 or similar frameworks) is a plus. Proactive mindset with a focus on continuous improvement and risk mitigation.
descriptionByLocale.de:
**Metall- und Inventarcontroller** — MKS PAMP SA, Castel San Pietro (TI).

**MISSION** Der Metall- und Inventarcontroller ist für die Gewährleistung einer robusten Kontrollentwicklung und -durchführung bei Metall- und Materialbewegungen, Inventurgenauigkeit und damit verbundenen Prozessen in der Fabrikumgebung verantwortlich. Als zweite Kontrollinstanz überprüft der Mitarbeiter unabhängig, validiert und testet Materialflüsse und Inventurtransaktionen, um die Einhaltung von internen Richtlinien und Verfahren sicherzustellen. Die Position trägt zum kontinuierlichen Verbesserung von Inventur- und Bewegungscontrollen bei, unterstützt die zweijährliche physische Inventur und liefert handlungsfähige Einblicke, um die operative Integrität, Transparenz und Risikominderung zu stärken. **HAUPTAKTIVITÄTEN UND VERANTWORTUNGEN** Kontrollentwicklung und -durchführung Unterstützung bei der Entwicklung, Dokumentation, Auditierung und Wartung von Kontrollen im Zusammenhang mit Metall-/Materialbewegungen und Inventurverfolgung. Durchführung regelmäßiger Überprüfungen der Kontrollen, um Lücken oder Schwächen zu identifizieren. Durchführung detaillierter Tests von Bewegungen und Anpassungen, um die Einhaltung der dokumentierten Verfahren zu bestätigen. Koordination mit der Produktion, den Betriebsabteilungen und der Logistik, um Bewegungsdisparitäten zu klären und Kontrollstandards durchzusetzen. Inventurüberwachung und -abrechnung Überwachung von Eingangs-/Ausgangsmetallbewegungen und Validierung der Genauigkeit in Inventarsystemen. Abrechnung von Inventurakten mit physischen Zählungen und Systemtransaktionen, um Varianzen zu identifizieren. Durchführung von Ursachenanalysen von Disparitäten und Koordination von korrektiven Maßnahmen. Unterstützung von Zählprogrammen und Leitung von Untersuchungen zu den Ursachen von Zählunterschieden. Physische Inventurunterstützung Unterstützung bei der Planung, Durchführung und Dokumentation von physischen Inventuren. Unterstützung bei der Vorbereitung von Inventurzählverfahren, Kontrolltestkriterien und Berichten. Prozessverbesserung und Governance Vorschläge zur Verbesserung von Richtlinien, Verfahren und Systemkontrollen, um die Inventurgenauigkeit und die Kontrollentwicklung zu stärken. Zusammenarbeit mit den Produktions-, Logistik- und IT-Teams, um Prozesse zu straffen und Fehlerquoten zu reduzieren. Überprüfung von Prozessflüssen, Kontrollen und Schulungsmaterialien für die physischen Bewegungen von Metallen. Berichterstattung und Stakeholder-Engagement Vorbereitung von Berichten über Inventurgenauigkeit, Kontrollverstöße, Testergebnisse und Verbesserungsinitiativen. Funktion als Schlüsselfunktion zwischen Qualitätssicherung, Betriebsabteilungen, IT und Fabrikleitung bei Metallbewegungen/Inventur- und Kontrollfragen. Unterstützung von internen/externen Audits mit Beweisen, Rechnungen und Kontrollentwicklungsunterlagen. **ANFORDERUNGEN, FÄHIGKEITEN UND ERFAHRUNGEN** 5-10 Jahre relevante Erfahrung in der Industriellen Ingenieurwesen, Supply Chain Management, Qualitätssicherung oder Logistik. Erfahrung in Inventurkontrolle, Materialkontrolle oder einer damit verbundenen Funktion in einer Fertigungs- oder Fabrikumgebung. Bachelor-Abschluss in Industriellen Ingenieurwesen, Supply Chain Management, Qualitätssicherung oder Logistik (wünschenswert). Erfahrung in der Kontrollentwicklung, Inventurabrechnung und Prozessverbesserung. Praktische Erfahrung mit ERP- und Inventarsystemen (z.B. SAP, Oracle oder ähnlichen). **BESONDERE QUALIFIKATIONEN, FÄHIGKEITEN UND ERFAHRUNGEN** Italienisch ist obligatorisch, konversatorisch in Englisch. Starkes analytisches, untersuchendes und problemlösungsfähiges Können. Exzellente Aufmerksamkeit zur Einzelheit und Genauigkeit in der Berichterstattung und Datenprüfung. Fähigkeit, unabhängig zu arbeiten, Prioritäten zu setzen und Ergebnisse klar und übersichtlich an Funktionen zu kommunizieren. Kenntnisse von Qualitätskontrollstandards und -best Practices (z.B. ISO 9001 oder ähnliche Rahmenwerke) sind ein Plus. Proaktives Denken mit Schwerpunkt auf kontinuierlicher Verbesserung und Risikominderung.
descriptionByLocale.fr: Contrôleur métal et inventaire — MKS PAMP SA, Castel San Pietro (TI). MISSION Le Contrôleur métal et inventaire est responsable de l'assurance d'une conception de contrôle robuste et d'une efficacité opérationnelle dans les mouvements de métal et de matériaux, l'exactitude de l'inventaire et les processus connexes au sein de l'environnement de la fabrique. En tant que deuxième ligne de contrôle, le rôle effectue des revues, des validations et des tests indépendants des flux de matériaux et des transactions d'inventaire pour s'assurer de la conformité aux politiques et procédures internes. Le poste contribue à l'amélioration continue des contrôles d'inventaire et de mouvement, soutient les comptes physiques bi-annuels de l'inventaire et fournit des informations actionnables pour renforcer l'intégrité opérationnelle, la transparence et la mitigation des risques. ACTIVITÉS ET RESPONSABILITÉS PRINCIPALES Conception de contrôle et efficacité opérationnelle Soutien à la conception, à la documentation, à l'audit et à la maintenance des contrôles liés aux mouvements de métal/matériaux et à la gestion de l'inventaire. Effectuer des revues régulières de l'efficacité opérationnelle des contrôles, identifier les lacunes ou les faiblesses. Effectuer des tests détaillés des mouvements et des ajustements pour confirmer l'adhésion aux procédures documentées. Coordination avec la production, les opérations et la logistique pour clarifier les désaccords sur les mouvements et imposer les normes de contrôle. Suivi de l'inventaire et réconciliation Suivi des mouvements métal entrants/sortants et validation de l'exactitude des systèmes d'inventaire. Réconciliation des enregistrements d'inventaire avec les comptes physiques et les transactions système pour identifier les écarts. Analyse des causes des désaccords et coordination des actions correctives. Soutien aux programmes de comptage cyclique et conduite des investigations des causes profondes des différences de comptage. Soutien aux comptes physiques Soutien à la planification, à l'exécution et à la documentation des comptes physiques. Soutien à la préparation des procédures de comptage d'inventaire, des critères de test et des rapports. Amélioration des processus et gouvernance Proposition d'améliorations aux politiques, aux procédures et aux contrôles de système pour renforcer l'exactitude de l'inventaire et la conception de contrôle. Collaboration avec les équipes de production, de logistique et d'IT pour simplifier les processus et réduire les taux d'erreur. Revue des flux de processus, des contrôles et des matériaux de formation pour les mouvements physiques de métaux. RAPPORTS ET ENGAGEMENT DES PARTENAIRES Préparation de rapports sur l'exactitude de l'inventaire, les déviations de contrôle, les résultats des tests et les initiatives d'amélioration. Agir en tant qu'interface clé entre la qualité, les opérations, l'IT et la direction de la fabrique sur les questions de mouvement de métal / inventaire et de contrôle. Soutien aux audits internes/externes avec des preuves, des réconciliations et des documents de conception de contrôle. FORMATION ET EXPÉRIENCE REQUISES 5-10 ans d'expérience pertinente dans l'ingénierie industrielle, la gestion de la chaîne d'approvisionnement, la gestion de la qualité ou la logistique. Expérience prouvée dans le contrôle de l'inventaire, le contrôle des matériaux ou une fonction connexe dans un environnement de fabrication ou de fabrique. Diplôme universitaire en ingénierie industrielle, gestion de la chaîne d'approvisionnement, gestion de la qualité ou logistique (préféré). Expérience démontrée dans la conception de contrôle, la réconciliation de l'inventaire et l'amélioration des processus. Expérience pratique avec les systèmes ERP et de gestion d'inventaire (par exemple, SAP, Oracle ou équivalent). COMPÉTENCES, COMPÉTENCES ET EXPÉRIENCE Langue italienne obligatoire, conversationnel en anglais. Capacité analytique, investigative et de résolution de problèmes solide. Excellente attention aux détails et précision dans les rapports et les revues de données. Capacité à travailler de manière indépendante, à prioriser les tâches et à communiquer les résultats clairement au travers des fonctions. Connaissance des normes de contrôle de qualité et des meilleures pratiques (par exemple, ISO 9001 ou cadres similaires) est un plus. Esprit proactif avec un focus sur l'amélioration continue et la mitigation des risques.

jobs.title: Specialista tecnico chimico responsabile conduzione impianto
jobs.description:
Specialista tecnico chimico responsabile conduzione impianto — MKS PAMP SA, Castel San Pietro (TI).

MISSIONE La risorsa si occuperà della supervisione e della conduzione di impianti chimici e dei relativi sistemi di automazione, garantendo stabilità operativa, la corretta applicazione delle strategie di controllo. Sarà responsabile del monitoraggio continuo dei parametri di processo e della pronta gestione di eventuali deviazioni, sia di natura impiantistica sia legate ai sistemi di automazione, collaborando con le funzioni tecniche per assicurare efficienza, sicurezza e continuità operativa. PRINCIPALI ATTIVITÀ E RESPONSABILITÀ Supportare e supervisionare l’operatività quotidiana dell’impianto durante il turno; Agire come primo livello di risposta per problematiche di automazione e strumentazione; Interpretare allarmi, interblocchi e comportamento del processo; Garantire la corretta applicazione delle SOP e della filosofia di controllo; Effettuare le campionature di processo e interpretare i relativi dati; Collaborare attivamente con gli operatori interni, favorendo lo scambio di competenze e lapprendimento reciproco; Collaborare con altri enti aziendali nell’ottica del miglioramento continuo; Garantire ordine, pulizia e una corretta gestione del passaggio di consegne tra turni. FORMAZIONE ED ESPERIENZA RICHIESTE Specialista tecnico Chimico; Preferibile esperienza pregressa nel settore farmaceutico; Solida conoscenza di impianti automatizzati con PLC/HMI; Buona conoscenza di strumentazione e loop di controllo; La conoscenza della lingua inglese è considerata un plus. QUALIFICHE E COMPETENZE Senso di responsabilità e iniziativa; Capacità di lavorare in autonomia; Capacità di analisi e problem solving in contesti produttivi complessi, con elevata attenzione ai dettagli; Resistenza allo stress e alla pressione del tempo, con capacità di gestire efficacemente periodi di massima attività; Mentalità agile e flessibile, con approccio reattivo alle richieste operative; Capacità di interagire con colleghi di diverse culture; Disponibilità a lavorare su turni e a operare direttamente sullimpianto; Cittadino svizzero o dellUE o titolare di un permesso di lavoro svizzero valido.
